methods.method_name: Histochemical Analysis of GUS Expression in Plants and Pollen
methods.description: This method describes the process for assaying GUS activity in A. thaliana plants and pollen grains using X-Gluc substrate solution, followed by destaining and microscopic examination to visualize GUS expression.

steps.description: A. thaliana plants and pollen grains were immersed for 12-18 h in a X-Gluc substrate solution.
steps.searchable_summary: immerse plants and pollen in X-Gluc solution for GUS activity assay

steps.description: Assayed plants were extracted with ethanol to remove chlorophyll and improve visibility of the blue GUS reaction product.
steps.searchable_summary: extract chlorophyll from plants using ethanol

steps.description: Destained plants were examined under a dissection microscope.
Plant organs and pollen grains were mounted in water between slides and coverslips for observation at higher magnifications.
steps.searchable_summary: mount plant organs and pollen for high magnification observation

methods.method_name: Visualization of Transient GUS Expression in Pollen Tubes
methods.description: This method outlines the procedure for visualizing transient GUS expression in pollen tubes grown from bombarded grains using X-Gluc solution and microscopy.

steps.description: X-Gluc solution (1 ml per plate) was evenly distributed on the surface of solid culture medium covered with pollen tubes grown from bombarded grains.
steps.searchable_summary: apply X-Gluc solution to culture medium with pollen tubes

steps.description: After 2 h of incubation, squares of solid medium were cut out and flipped upside-down (pollen tubes in direct contact with the glass) onto coverslips.
steps.searchable_summary: incubate and prepare pollen tubes for microscopy

steps.description: Stained plant organs, pollen grains, and pollen tubes were analyzed by bright-field transmitted light microscopy using an Axioscope (Carl Zeiss Inc.) microscope.
Images were taken by 35-mm photography (64T film; Eastman Kodak Co.).

methods.method_name: Preparation of X-Gluc Solutions for GUS Assay
methods.description: This method details the preparation of X-Gluc solutions for incubating plants and pollen tubes to assay GUS activity, including specific concentrations and conditions.

steps.description: Incubation in X-Gluc solution was performed at 37 Њ C. All X-Gluc solutions were buffered with 0.1 M sodium phosphate at pH 7.0.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare X-Gluc solution with sodium phosphate buffer

steps.description: Plants were incubated in a solution containing 0.2% X-Gluc (Jersey Lab and Gloves Supply), 0.1% Triton X-100, 5 mM potassium ferricyanide, 5 mM potassium ferrocyanide, and 6% N, N-dimethyl-formamide (DMF).
The same solution supplemented with 5% mannitol was used for pollen tubes.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare incubation solution for pollen tubes with mannitol

steps.description: Pollen grains were assayed in 0.1% X-Gluc, 0.1% Triton X-100, 3% DMF, and 5% mannitol.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare assay solution for pollen grains
